candidate_problems.text: What concrete principle could make quantum vacuum contributions fail to gravitate generically while preserving the empirical successes of effective field theory and general relativity elsewhere? The open tension is to formulate a non-ad hoc mechanism—symmetry, sequestering, emergent gravity, or something else—whose parts are genuinely load-bearing and not endlessly retunable when field content, cutoffs, or phase transitions change.

Why is the observed acceleration so well fit by a tiny positive cosmological constant with w≈-1 and little sign of clustering or time variation, yet its onset occurs on the present cosmological timescale? The unresolved problem is whether this is a deep dynamical/boundary-condition explanation, a selection effect, or a coincidence that signals missing structure in cosmology.

Can anthropic or multiverse explanations be turned into genuinely explanatory, testable selection structures rather than post hoc accommodation? The tension is whether such accounts can derive a constrained distribution for the cosmological constant tied to galaxy formation, observers, and perhaps other correlated observables, instead of merely allowing the observed value somewhere in a vast landscape.
